top_categories.workout_context: 16,400ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
At mile 60 of 130, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Sportful Dolomiti Race's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
126ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 130 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Sportful Dolomiti Race. Train it until it feels boring.
Sportful Dolomiti Race's paved Dolomite mountain passes forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 130 miles.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 16,400ft of climbing on paved Dolomite mountain passes does to your power file on race day.
At Sportful Dolomiti Race, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
130 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved Dolomite mountain passes. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 130 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Sportful Dolomiti Race’s 130 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Sportful Dolomiti Race with 16,400ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%. Manghen summit (2,047m): 8-12C with wind — the Dolomites' exposed upper reaches; Dolomite thunderstorms build rapidly — lightning at altitude is a genuine hazard.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Durability, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 130 miles of paved dolomite mountain passes in Feltre, Belluno, Veneto, Italy.
